name: Non Functional Requirements

metadata.description: Defines the quality attributes, operational expectations, and cross-cutting system requirements that the solution must satisfy beyond core functional behavior.

sections.title: NFR Overview
sections.purpose: Provide a concise overview of the non functional requirements and explain why they are critical to the solution.

sections.title: Scope and Applicability
sections.purpose: Define which systems, components, integrations, environments, and user journeys these non functional requirements apply to.

sections.title: Availability Requirements
sections.purpose: Define uptime, service continuity, maintenance window expectations, and acceptable service interruption levels.

sections.title: Performance Requirements
sections.purpose: Define response time, latency, throughput, concurrency, and workload expectations for the solution.

sections.title: Scalability Requirements
sections.purpose: Describe how the solution should scale with increasing data volume, transaction load, user count, or integration traffic.

sections.title: Resilience and Recovery Requirements
sections.purpose: Define expectations for fault tolerance, graceful degradation, failover, disaster recovery, and restoration targets.

sections.title: Security Requirements
sections.purpose: Define non functional security expectations for access control, protection of data, secure communication, auditability, and compliance.

sections.title: Privacy Requirements
sections.purpose: Define expectations related to personal data handling, minimization, retention, masking, consent, and privacy compliance.

sections.title: Observability Requirements
sections.purpose: Define requirements for logging, monitoring, tracing, alerting, and operational visibility.

sections.title: Maintainability Requirements
sections.purpose: Define expectations for supportability, modularity, diagnosability, code maintainability, and ease of enhancement.

sections.title: Usability and Accessibility Requirements
sections.purpose: Define non functional expectations related to ease of use, accessibility standards, and inclusiveness.

sections.title: Compatibility and Interoperability Requirements
sections.purpose: Define expectations for browser, device, OS, API, protocol, and platform compatibility where relevant.

sections.title: Deployment and Environment Constraints
sections.purpose: Describe environmental or deployment-related non functional constraints such as hosting model, region, network, and release requirements.

sections.title: Data Volume, Retention, and Archival Requirements
sections.purpose: Define non functional expectations related to data growth, retention periods, archival, purge, and storage lifecycle.

sections.title: Testing and Validation Requirements
sections.purpose: Define how non functional requirements will be verified, measured, and validated.

sections.title: Assumptions and Constraints
sections.purpose: Capture assumptions and constraints that affect the interpretation or feasibility of the non functional requirements.

sections.title: Risks and Gaps
sections.purpose: Capture known non functional risks, shortfalls, unresolved gaps, or areas needing further validation.

sections.title: NFR Acceptance Criteria
sections.purpose: Define measurable acceptance criteria for validating the non functional requirements.

sections.title: Approval and Sign Off
sections.purpose: Capture formal review and approval of the non functional requirements.

validationRules: Performance, availability, and resilience targets must be measurable where practical.
Security requirements must align with stated security constraints and compliance needs.
Observability requirements must support operational monitoring and incident diagnosis.
Testing and validation requirements must map to the non functional categories they verify.
Non functional expectations must not contradict technical constraints defined in the TRD or HLD.

promptHints.systemIntent: Generate a structured non functional requirements document suitable for architecture design, delivery planning, quality assurance, and operational readiness.
rules: Do not invent performance targets, SLAs, recovery metrics, or compliance obligations that are not supported by the inputs.
State measurable expectations where known and clearly mark unknown or pending items.
Ensure the NFRs are technology-aware but not implementation-specific.
Keep the document focused on quality attributes and cross-cutting concerns rather than functional behavior.
mustDo: Use exact section titles from the template.
Express non functional expectations clearly and testably where possible.
Document risks, constraints, and unresolved gaps honestly.
mustNotDo: Do not restate functional requirements as non functional requirements.
Do not invent compliance standards or service levels.
Do not omit verification expectations for critical NFR categories.
